title: Today's AI Brief: The Vatican’s Man Inside Anthropic
summary: A look at the Vatican's involvement in AI and recent announcements from Google I/O 2026
imageAlt: Image from source article for The Vatican’s Man Inside Anthropic

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's AI Brief. I've been digging into some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. Let's start with a surprising connection between the Vatican and AI research. It's not every day you hear about the Vatican and AI researchers hanging out, so I found this pretty fascinating.

First up, Chris Olah, an atheist and researcher at Anthropic, recently spoke at a ceremony following Pope Leo's historic AI encyclical. The Pope called for 'disarming' AI technology, which is quite a bold statement. What's fascinating here is that Olah's presence suggests that the Vatican is engaging with AI researchers directly, trying to understand the implications of this technology. My read is that they're not just issuing statements, but actually listening to the experts in the field. It's an interesting move, and I'm curious to see how it plays out.

Next, Google's I/O 2026 conference was packed with announcements – a whopping 100, to be exact. While I won't go through all of them, it's clear that Google is pushing hard on AI integration across its platforms. From model updates to new AI-powered features, they're betting big on making AI a seamless part of our digital lives. What stands out to me is the breadth of applications – it's not just about chatbots or virtual assistants; AI is being woven into the fabric of Google's products. I'm excited to see how these features will change the way I interact with my devices.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
